mappings.rationaleAI: Mineralogy covering major mineral families, crystal structures, and geochemical analysis addresses identification and distribution of natural resources (A.1) and mining products including metal ores (B.1.b).

Geology covering Earth's structure, resource discovery, and exploitation including minerals, fossil fuels, and water addresses resource identification (A.1), mining and quarrying (B.1), and petroleum and natural gas (B.2).

Geology of igneous, sedimentary, and metamorphic rocks connecting petrology to resource extraction addresses resource identification (A.1), mining products (B.1), and processing of stone, sand, clay, and gravel (C.5).

Materials science studying metals, ceramics, and polymers covering fabrication processes with gold, cement, sand, and carbon connects to metal ore processing (C.3), glass manufacture (C.5.a), cement manufacture (C.5.b), and industrial ceramics (C.5.c).

Chemical elements covering atomic structure, the periodic table, and cultural significance of elements like gold and oxygen relate to production and processing of metals (C.3) and processing of water to obtain elements (C.6).
Industrial chemistry applications including fuels, power generation, and materials connect to primary conversion processes generally (C), petroleum refining (C.1), and coal utilization (C.2).
Petrochemical applications and pharmaceutical chemistry connect to petroleum refining (C.1) and processing plant and animal products to obtain pharmaceuticals (C.8.g).
Isotope applications in nuclear power generation and geochronology connect to uranium processing (C.3.s) as a key nuclear fuel material.
Sustainability covering material flows, renewability, and circularity directly addresses management and conservation of natural resources (A.2, A.2.a).
Forest ecosystem services including timber and carbon cycling address resource identification (A.1), conservation (A.2.a), and processing plant products to obtain wood (C.8.b).
Crystal structure determination with applications in materials science and drug design connects to production of synthetic gemstones and industrial crystals (C.4).
Industrialization innovations in iron production by Darby and steam power connect to mining (B.1), petroleum and coal utilization (C.1, C.2), and metal processing (C.3).
Physical and chemical properties of water across its three states relate to processing of water to obtain elements including hydrogen and oxygen (C.6).
Conservation strategies addressing habitat loss and biodiversity decline directly relate to conservation of natural resources (A.2.a).
Economic analysis of environmental policy and pollution control addresses management of resources (A.2) and conservation of natural resources (A.2.a).
Decarbonization solutions and the restructuring of industrial economies relate to conservation of natural resources (A.2.a) and petroleum refining's environmental context (C.1).
Engineering analysis covering fossil fuels, bioenergy, and nuclear power addresses petroleum and natural gas production (B.2), petroleum refining (C.1), and coal utilization (C.2).
